mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating jobless claims signal deteriorating labor market conditions, which compress consumer spending expectations and drive negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative returns more than 55% of the time over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production paired with stable consumer prices indicates benign supply-side expansion that boosts corporate margins and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in > 50% of instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between INDPRO growth and forward SPX returns drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over the subsequent week as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of observations, breaking the earnings-growth linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 5.0% trigger valuation compression and higher borrowing costs for leveraged corporate sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain stable or expand when FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0% for consecutive quarters, indicating a regime shift where rate sensitivity has broken down.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and a stable unemployment rate signals a tight labor market that supports wage-driven consumption, lifting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns in > 55% of instances, or payroll growth decouples from consumption spending and fails to translate into equity outperformance.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader consumer confidence and durable goods demand, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following a >5% monthly drop in housing starts, indicating the housing signal has lost its leading indicator status.
